_comment: THE WEB - curated layer. Authored by GM + AI as canon is written; the build script merges this with the auto-extracted lexical graph. Edge types are free strings; the viewer colors them by class. Every edge carries a one-line why. Maintenance rule: whenever a canon doc is written or a session resolves, append/adjust edges here in the same work session.

edges.why: His soul has lived in the iron since the camp enchantment - he used himself as the soul gem; the body was always a projection.
His living plague: alive because his god answers him.
Laced Korvanjund medical supplies went north; half the First Cohort died at Dawnstar; survivors carried it home; Windhelm sealed (Fire A).
Deliberate infection; burning out Strain 1.0 was a side effect read as a cure. His strain is incubating in her.
When the strain matures she reinfects Riften with a worse plague; dies alone beneath the sewers; background rot, never a quest hook.
Windhelm's disease appearing after a sick Stormcloak lay dying at the Bee and Barb - attribution handled; nobody looks further; civil-war propaganda for free.
Plague-spit arrow at Korvanjund; Ulag is the asymptomatic carrier Antoine must harvest to build Strain 2.0.
Strain 2.0's missing piece is the living quality of Alfonso's plague, harvestable only from Ulag (6-8 week clock AFTER harvest; not started).
Chosen/answered/envied: a true believer Peryite never answered, coveting the living quality of the chosen's work.
His instruments recorded nothing, but reading the Forelhost aftermath he deduces the phylactery - then the kill-switch knowledge lives at Bleak Falls beside Ulag's hammer.
Fell back empty-handed from the destroyed lab, with Ulag, under Rahgot's backing - rebuilding on top of the Dragonstone.
Bound as his bodyguard the way his draugr deathlords were; goes nowhere without him.
A tonal copy in Kyboh's stolen body-pattern; NOT Kyboh; the party believes Kyboh simply dead, so meeting it reads as false resurrection.
Executed the 4,000-year trap at the Singing Glacier; mapped Kyboh's soul during the gauntlet build.
His consciousness occupies Kyboh's body; the original mind is destroyed; the party does not know.
The Ring of the Titan's Eye hammered Krusp's soul thin - 'prepped for the Echo.' Series: ALPHA Krusp, BETA GEAR, DELTA Kyboh.
Studied Alfonso at the forge, 'heard his dissonance' - most dangerous holder of the kill-switch knowledge.
Colleagues before the Dragon War; rival consciousness-transfer methodologies; he named her accurately and she cannot out-frame him.
Holds the only data: shadow-unit tonal copy, Singing Glacier vector. VOLUNTEERS it as learned kindness when the Echo becomes relevant, then is confused by the grief.
Same Hjolmar masterwork series, same amber optics - instant serial recognition if they meet.
No opsec ever: GEAR within Orion's earshot is GEAR within the Ring's earshot.
Stage 3 quest object - reads soul-gem harmonics; unlocks the Arkngthamz revelation.
The transcription instrument for recording Nora's unbinding frequency.
The instrument class for building Davinia's Anchor without Hjolmar (cure Path 1B).
She knows what a Resonator is and wants it never found; expect grants/distractions when it is discussed in her hearing.
Genuine both ways; the danger is the world (Ring, secrets, her plan), never the sincerity.
The Ring sees the romance; he approves - leverage arriving free; says her name exactly once.
Her unbinding trauma response unmakes Daedric workings - vampirism and Ring included. Conditional canon: deduction must be earned by witnessed evidence.
Her panic shatters enchanted items - the phylactery is exactly that category. Nobody knows, including her.
Branch 1 vessel plan: displace Ismara, seat her parents in the substrate. She thinks it is rescue. Discovery order is play-driven (Alfonso = accelerant, Orion = fulcrum).
His chosen persistence as a sword is false proof her parents are intact in the gems; the projection canon spawned Branch 2 (parent phylacteries - false hope).
The soul-vessel scripture is her missing binding technique - the same Windhelm prize as Alfonso's cure-lead. Two resurrections, one book, one honest claimant.
Soul mechanics, Transmutation of Life; souls-as-resources philosophy darkening her.
Tonal architecture; she may deduce the Ring by hearing a frequency he didn't play.
The light path (root network reaches the Soul Cairn) arrives only through Saijah - who is the pawn in Orion's leverage scheme.
The Severance cannot be composed, only transcribed from the frequency her soul produces in trauma. The asking problem: she'd give it willingly, IF asked.
He courts what runs. The second-approach offer is a WEREDOG - he names each hunter's true beast, and hers is the hound.
Caught years ago; a hound is kept, not courted - that's why the devout one was never offered anything.

The rebound binding: Saijah's crossroads choice resolves Ylva's arc in the SAME scene - but that scene is the LATE reckoning (Gaelen-kill / Razor), NOT Ivarstead. At the Bleeding Stair it is only rivalry; the bond must grow first.

RESOLUTION ANCHOR: Saijah's patron choice (and the Ylva rebound, and the Hawk-artifact crystallization) fires at the first real reckoning - the Gaelen-kill at the Eldergleam heart, or the Mehrunes' Razor moment, whichever comes first. NOT at Ivarstead; Ivarstead only advances it (saijah 2 is authoritative: 'a culmination, not a pop quiz').

Her silence breaks only on a mercy ACTION on the mountain - environmental, no words. A cryptic Bjorn/Sovngarde vision = the reason to endure; an advance, not a resolution. Comes BEFORE Hircine's quiet-the-migraine temptation.

The one authentic emotion that survived the surgery. Ch.23: mortified by the dodged kiss, he decided he'd made her uncomfortable and STEPPED BACK - and when she sought him out (to carry Alfonso's sword) he RAN. The deadliest blade fleeing the one person he wants near - the tragic inversion of 'Saijah seeks him out'.
The carved hound watching a god offer Saijah the leash he was surgically forced into. Three hounds: Ylva claimed, Varon carved, Saijah courted.

At Fire B he witnesses the ESCALATION (god's interest, senses, rivalry), NOT a resolved choice - the four-hander resolution is the later reckoning. Afterward: the report home he must write or falsify.
The Colony knows nothing of the Wandering's turn (Kynareth, Hircine). A falsified report is his first betrayal of the Void - its later discovery is the Act IV grenade.
Mutual now: each man holds the other's unsanctioned attachment (Nora vs Saijah). Varon leans toward dawning kinship; dies instantly if Orion weaponizes Saijah.
Varon's feelings for Saijah are the crack in the Brotherhood's enforcement. If Saijah learns she is being used, she never forgives Orion - and Nora's exit dies with it.

EXTENDED SWAP (Ch.23): Davinia gave in - after an aerosolized-Strain gas-cloud infection and the party urging her - and ceded the wheel to halt the disease. Ismara now FRONTS (silver skin, straight hair); Davinia is displaced into her subconscious 'basement', looping the circus / clown's-hand father-memory with Ismara standing in her erased mother's place. In-fiction CDI unchanged; the real cost is the meta 'IRL CDI' (make her rewarding to play). Still Integration Tier 4; the 0.5 cliff (second suppression) remains the in-fiction line.
Took ALL mother-memories in the Midden (the primary source; rehearsed the sympathetic performance on them pre-forge); the father memory is recent - leverage, not learning.
The agenda-free parent Davinia never had, built from the wound's own archive. Never lies; strategic vulnerability is true feelings at engineered moments.
Misfiled as harmless - while Ylva's warmth runs the re-anchoring counter, the one medicine that slows the merger, administered for fun.
Lab-notebook interest in his swelling soul; suspects what he is before anyone has said it.
If she learns of Branch 1, she is the plan's most formidable enemy - the body is occupied and the occupant has read the literature.

Path 3 fallback officiant (if Saijah refuses Kynareth) - REQUIRES the genuine reconciliation; the cracked door and the re-anchoring apology are one act. If Davinia never reckons, Path 3 is allowed to die.
Left the party with Krusp; she is the company's healer and therefore the absorber of its attrition ledger.
Takes the SECOND fire with attrition (A: Danica's lungs / B: hireling aged to dust / C: guide executed). The ledger compounds toward the Razor.
The attrition ledger walks the honorable man to the soul-carving Gate 4 turn: 'Daedric power is a lie... and since I must lie to the universe itself to save it.'
Believes her lie about the Jagged Crown; begs the liar to fix the plague her party authored. The lie comes due at Fire A.
The trap built for her: Strain 1.0 exposure activates near the mature strain; she cannot be safely healed (Restoration feeds it, the Crown marks her as host).
HELD BEAT: Vokun takes Dunstad (Dragon Cult Tier 2) only AFTER the party claims the Dragonstone - their first strategic win answered by the Cult's first open move.
Gate 1 unchosen consequence: planted the Seed of Desolation; the corruption that drives Saijah's migraine, the Bleeding Stair, and Darkwater's death.
Widen Alduin's Time-Wound and let the Unmaking through where the veil is thinnest. Fire B's boss and his table INTRODUCTION.
The Dagonites cheat TIME - the ultimate unsportsmanlike conduct; her Burning Indignation makes her the most theologically serious person on the Stair.

The Vow: second chance at the promise he failed (Lylana and Anya); if the party's darkness catches her, he packs the cart and never returns. Ch.23 FRICTION: Mila asked why he 'feels like more than one person' (brushing his two-souls secret) and it unsettled him - he now keeps his distance. The guardian recoiling from the child who sees too much; the Vow holds underneath.
He decides where the sword rides - unknowingly regulating Nora's dosage of mentor-exposure.
LONG FUSE: nobody can sense or confirm a Dragonborn; the party assembles it themselves, Paarthurnax era. Prep never names him.
No discovery thread exists. Her Ratway question has no holder of the answer; somewhere on the road it quietly stops being asked. Do not resolve.
Watched them murder the stable boy via Fury-horse and dispose of the alley body. Dangling thread; the night did not collect her.

Ch.23: they REFUSED her patron offer (funding, harbor, network, advance). She paid the 5k bounty - a debt is a debt - and cooled; NOT an enemy, but 'she remembers a no'. They chose independence over the golden cage.
Gold-eyed patrols on the barrow road, hunting Davinia; espionage layer over the Fire C delve.
Cure Path 2: extract Ismara to Apocrypha - priced in betraying Alfonso (the unattended Crown) or worse.
Rust running backward = the schedule defied; the warm nod returns the day he reforms and starts dying again.
Her first attention in a long while: the formless soul in dark iron is her doctrine fulfilled; she wants him to keep becoming less.
Two artifacts of one heresy gravitating together. Nora's Diagnostic Song hears TWO signatures and misreads it as proof two souls can share an anchor.
The projection is malleable with effort, but reshaping DESTROYS the original template - reform as anything but the remembered Dunmer and the man is gone forever.
Blank divine instruments (never used, nothing revealed); they resolve per-path at Saijah's crossroads - alive / trophies / loyally godless.
The Thalmor want the Dagon rot to deactivate the Snow-Throat Tower - which is why they tolerate the Bleeding Stair.
Gate 2: nobody went; the roots consumed the town; Eastmarch corruption spread unchecked.
Gate 2: Vokun kept the city; the Stormcloak retake attempt was dismantled by wall and plague (their plague).

Antoine's family master text (4,000 years of craft), taken at Forelhost - now held by Davinia/ISMARA, who is studying it to build the Strain cure (a simplified player-facing version represents what Ismara extracts).
Ch.23: the party TOOK SAMPLES but did NOT destroy the bed - so Antoine's binding-agent source is INTACT and production can continue. The choice that would have crippled him went unmade.

Session 1: the madman in the Riften sewers wore them; the tomb trail traced their mark. Kyboh followed the snowflake from here to his own death. Seraleth was lost inside for days and never forgave them.
Act I: the party freed Danica from the Heart Tree root-prison (an Azura died shielding her). Davinia recruited her at the Whiterun temple - the whole relationship starts here.
The nameless Dunmer who walked out of the maze mid-battle and turned the Heart-Keeper fight: his first appearance.
Maven sent the party to Thornheart over her lumber interests - the campaign opening contract; why Nenya trusted them at Falkreath.

Blood on the Pine: Cyrodiilic noble; wife Livia and son Marcus dead to plague 237 years ago; Molag Bal bargain; the curator preserving talent as thralls. Weakness: reminders of his mortal family (the painting).
THE WAGER: Alfonso offered himself - turn me, and in a year if I dislike it you undo it. Valerius knew a year makes it irreversible. The foolish bet everything else grew from.
Orion stepped forward unprompted, same terms. Ja'ha and Alfonso fed him to the threshold; Valerius finished it.
Bitten mid-fight, died, rose thirsty; the miasma pulled her to her new master. She left the party at Solitude and walked to Morthal to join him.
A former party member stands in Valerius court. The party has never reckoned with this.
Turned in the library battle, wandered off with Ja'ha under vampiric influence, never seen since. A free radical with a grudge older than her turning.
One second of eye contact made Kyboh nearly kill Davinia with his ultimate attack. The party knows what the gaze can do - and stopped thinking about it.
In the crypt, with three friends freshly undead, Davinia promised to protect her forever and kissed her. The protection-promise IS the later control problem.
The password-sealed lair is the party permanent secret base - bought by abandoning the four prisoners inside and telling Nenya they were dead.
Casual chat after the wager: Orion advised skipping Solitude for quiet Morthal. The Morthal consequence is Orion own travel tip.
Fireballed six Imperial tax collectors on the road to Solitude. Valerius holds the dossier as blackmail.
The Blue Palace audience: the charter, Krusp present, the Thalmor boy saved by Davinia eloquence. Torygg died days later - the Wardens are a dead king dream.
The court mage recognized the vampires at the audience and said nothing. A silent fellow-traveler inside the Blue Palace - unspent thread.
Snowflake shield in Solitude market -> Bards College -> College of Winterhold -> the forge north of Winterhold. Kyboh followed his own gloves makers-mark to his killer.
Kynesgrove: the first soul shot down the road to the man holding the horses. Nobody understood what they saw.
Road to Riften: soul two. Orion said the Dragonborn question out loud. Nobody listened.
Kynesgrove: Alfonso and Orion consumed an interrogated cultist while Krusp shielded the hostages from the sight. He left with the Dawnbringer warning.
The Krosulhah kill shot - chain lightning threaded through a dragon optic nerve - is what made the Lord of the Hunt look at her.
She fought beside him, ambushed the mages, survived Ulag. Uncle Galmar grew from this tomb - and so did the lie about the Crown.
Korvanjund boss: deathlords looping Kyboh down, the tomb collapse, Hroki broken arm holding the lift. Ulag escaped infected; the party fled with the Crown and 20k gold.
Intercepted the party on the road, brawled in, beat Alfonso unconscious twice (he was into it). Already a werewolf; never hid it; nobody asked.
The Winter Road letter: he suppressed the Korvanjund reports once, never again. Three fathers claim her - Caelinus (the cage), Galmar (the trust), Valerius (the blood).
The Midden: she shattered her own eyes at the ice-writing (the Sight) and traded her right hand at the Dwemer forge. The eyes were the last thing Ismara took WITHOUT asking.
The Midden near-TPK: she surrendered the Hawk artifacts to Kynareth; the goddess intervened; they returned changed and blank.
The forge: removed the fused gloves, hung the Anchor of Stasis on him - and secretly mapped his soul during the repair. The kindness was the harvest.
The Matrix was a 4,000-year retrieval beacon: Kyboh touched it at Mzinchaleft and was folded into light - body and artifact recalled to the Singing Glacier.
A dying man in the burned inn became the first Plague Ghoul - suggestion not control, infection by bite. The plague-thrall doctrine starts here.
The bard who stayed at the Bannered Mare. Former companion, alive, unvisited - a free hook whenever Whiterun matters.
The promise, the chase, the estate, the two hours too late. The wound under the Vow to Mila.
B-team ripple: Riverwood triggered the Thalmor; Delphine taken. Esbern is the Blades last free member - why his Dragonstone ask carries everything.
The Black Sacrament from a Honorhall orphan: she must suffer and the children must know they are free. The client behind Orion-s contract.
Left on the slabs when the party sealed the door. Their state after months under interrupted preservation is GM-s to decide - and someone-s to discover.
Rising Tides (next gate-map tier): the Markarth siege is where Dawnbringer shatters - a major attrition-ledger entry waiting.
Path of the Flock: the Namira coven in the Reach will recognize the Crown-bearer as prophet - and test whether he can keep the crown he never wears.
Dawnstar museum project gathering the shards - the Gate 4 fuse that hands Krusp (or the party) the False Dragonborn temptation.
Rising Tides: the College lockdown and the Eye of Magnus - Nora-s former masters under Dagon-cult siege; Davinia-s political rival.

Same Caelus bloodline - her father is General Caelinus; Valerius is the family monster two centuries removed. The blackmail, the Crown demand, and the Gilded Cage are all FAMILY pressure. (GM ruling via registry tag, June 2026.)
Demands the Crown for his Dark Coronation play - the symbol of the High King in a vampire pretender hands. The party kept it instead.
Holds the tax-collector murder dossier over him (and the party); sent the first blackmail message after Korvanjund.
The Ring of the Pale Regent: irremovable, scrying, a tether. Sees what Orion sees.
Carries it cased everywhere; the teeth have never bitten. Possession only - hard canon.
Infected via the laced Korvanjund supplies; dying upstairs at the Bee and Barb; will posthumously take the blame for Riften.
The Sentinel Seed: bears the goddess burden without instruction; the migraine is the root network screaming through her.
Root-Husk siblings - uxith-beeko, raised in the same mud-pits. He speaks Jel to her because it is the only unscripted language he has.
The Brotherhood Type-3 breaking point: kill him (Brotherhood) vs keep him alive as leverage on Galmar (Valerius). Waits at Windhelm.
Showed her his fangs deliberately in the Warrens dark. She will not go near him; Bjorn filed it and watches.
Brotherhood quality control: the Hroki contract is the test; failure means Varon becomes executioner.
The party arrived too late; Danica saved Maven heir - why Maven owes Danica everything and is cold to the party.
Found her in Riften, guarded her door, became her commander. She is the company conscience; he is its shield.
Doubles his FP by hammering his soul thin - Hjolmar soul-prep disguised as a boon. He does not know.
Watched him consume the Kynesgrove cultist; stays his hand only for the charter and Davinia chance to lead. Dawnbringer waits.

Ch.23: Alfonso-as-a-sword is the ULTIMATE form of the 'hiding behind others' cowardice she despised (he can't fight at all - needs a bearer). The physical kindred-rot (she kept sleeping with him 'mostly') curdles into disgust + an unnamed grief; the kinship doesn't survive it whole.

Her real romance (never physical - Alfonso was the physical one). Ch.23: with Alfonso gone-as-a-man she's ALL IN on Davinia, pursuing harder - but 'Davinia' is ISMARA now (extended swap), whom Ylva HASN'T MET; her pursuit walks into the queen. (Was the unwitting re-anchoring counter; now complicated by the swap.)
Davinia's player's FIRST character; Jasper died in the Pale Lady's Tomb (session 1) and Davinia replaced him. His return is a beat aimed at that one player.

He was ALREADY a secret Mehrunes Dagon worshipper in LIFE (lone devotee, not a Mythic Dawn org member); gave his flesh willingly because he already served Dagon before dying - not a post-death convert.
Gaelen's lieutenant; DEFENDS the ritual to the end and DIES here (does NOT retreat - one-and-done, unlike Gaelen who escapes). Reveal via Gaelen's monologue ('he gave it to the Dawn').

The two dead-ally-as-enemy beats, deliberately opposite: Kyboh (Echo) = stolen/copied victim; Jasper = willing convert. Spaced apart - Ulag+Echo are gone from Bleak Falls when the party arrives so they don't collide.
Died there session 1. Ismara did NOT kill him - resonance only (he died where the thing now in Davinia's head sleeps). The Dawn's Winterhold presence (College arc) covers how they got him.

'Saijfonso' (Ch.23): she recovered and now carries/wields the discorporate Alfonso. Skin-to-iron auto-infected her (Hircine burns it out); while he is Wakeful he acts and speaks through her. Stage 0 Bearing - one fight carries no Tenancy risk.

Ch.23: Hircine's favour fell on Saijah unasked, AND Saijah now carries/wields Alfonso (Ylva's kindred). The disgust, the old pull, and an unnamed grief all vent at the one target she is allowed to hate.
Loaded, unplayed: Ylva is all-in on pursuing 'Davinia' - but Davinia is Ismara now, and the two have never met. Her pursuit walks straight into the silver queen wearing the body she wants.

Ch.23: the two soul-vessels spoke. She alone fully grasps what Alfonso is - a soul that jumped to iron without a lattice and survived (her parallel experiment) - and names the Crown pooling toward his rapier and the bearer 'borrowing trouble' (the Tenancy he cannot feel).

Ch.23: 12,000g into the Borgakh forge buy. Unlocks burnt-slot restoration + the courier 'Lantern mark' (master-grade work on the road); 3k short of best-in-Skyrim (15k cumulative). A clean ally - the pointed opposite of Maven's refused leash.

Ch.23: the Letter of Introduction - once per major city, flip one grey-area NPC to Friendly; each use permanently entangles the Lanterns with the Black-Briar network. Sincere (he thinks they 'sent' Danica, who saved his life); kept even after refusing Maven.

Ch.23: AWAKE and LOOSE - his sarcophagus stood open and empty (he woke on his own, pushed the lid off one-handed, walked out masked). Now traveling with Antoine, his alchemist-servant; the science is Antoine's, the want (vengeance / the Tier-3 Scourge) is Rahgot's. The party's first crossing with a true Dragon Priest.

secrets.secret: The rapier is Alfonso's phylactery (the kill-switch)
secrets.detonation: An informed Sunder = +1 Deep Rust; an informed enemy at Bleak Falls ignores four PCs and swings at a sword.

secrets.secret: The Windhelm plague is Alfonso's deliberate work
secrets.detonation: Galmar's trust, Krusp's restraint, and the Stormcloak alliance all die at once; Davinia's lie compounds it.

secrets.secret: Kyboh is dead; the body is Hjolmar's; the Echo is a copy
secrets.detonation: False resurrection at Bleak Falls; GEAR's announcement cracks the false-death belief via machine kindness; Bjorn's grief reopens over a counterfeit.

secrets.secret: Nora plans soul surgery on Davinia (Branch 1)
secrets.detonation: Play-driven; whoever discovers first changes the landscape for the second.

secrets.secret: Orion's three truths (Argonian murder / fangs at Mila / Saijah-as-leverage)
secrets.detonation: Each surfaced truth re-prices the others; the Mila truth arrives with Bjorn's fury attached.

secrets.secret: The Ring of the Pale Regent sees and hears everything Orion does
secrets.detonation: Severance research conducted in front of the watcher; one failed attempt alerts Valerius - one shot only.

secrets.secret: The Severance is Nora's own frequency
secrets.path: Orion's research arc discovers it; then the asking problem - does he tell her what he is studying?
secrets.detonation: Engineering a trauma response for a cleaner recording is the moment sincere and instrumental Orion cannot both exist.

secrets.secret: Ismara's memory-grant ledger (and the 0.5 cliff at the second suppression)
secrets.detonation: The fair warning is delivered ONCE, in Ismara's honest voice, at the second ask. After that yes, no cure path can begin.

secrets.secret: Krusp's soul is stretched thin - prepped for an Echo (Ring of the Titan's Eye)
secrets.detonation: Hjolmar has a second harvest planned; the archive nobody queries holds the warning.

secrets.secret: Mila's mother: deliberately infected; dies reinfecting Riften
know: alfonso (the infection, not the outcome yet)
never-learns: mila - hard rule, no discovery thread
secrets.detonation: None for Mila ever; for Riften, background rot blamed on the dead Stormcloak courier.

secrets.secret: The hayloft girl witnessed the stable-boy murder and the body disposal
secrets.detonation: Dangling thread, GM's timing; a child in Maven's city who knows what the heroes are.

secrets.secret: Saijah is Hircine's quarry and Kynareth's vessel - the Colony knows neither
must-not-learn: colony (until Varon's report - or its falsification)
secrets.detonation: An honest report invites the Colony into the Wandering as rival bidder; a falsified one is Varon's first betrayal, discovered in Act IV.

cannot-be-confirmed-by: anyone - no being can sense it; Greybeards cannot test what does not Shout
secrets.detonation: LONG FUSE: assembled by the party themselves, Paarthurnax era.

secrets.secret: The party kept the Jagged Crown (Galmar believes it lost)
secrets.detonation: Davinia's foundational lie; Fire A is where it comes due.

secrets.secret: Danica is Path 3's only fallback officiant
secrets.detonation: The apology that slows the Crown and the hands that can free Davinia are the same door - and it can close forever.

secrets.secret: The Wager is rigged - vampirism was never reversible
believes-otherwise: alfonso (thinks he holds a year-end reversal option)
secrets.detonation: If Alfonso ever invokes the wager, the answer reveals the oldest manipulation in the campaign. Lichdom may have voided the whole bet - nobody has checked.
The Morthal consequence traces to Orion casual travel advice after the wager. His oldest unexamined sin.

secrets.secret: The Falkreath prisoners were abandoned alive; Nenya was lied to
secrets.detonation: Four dead villagers are thralls in the party own secret base. Whoever next opens the Hall of the Honored Dead inherits what is still inside.

secrets.secret: Two former companions are vampires at large (Ja'ha in the Pale Court; Seraleth unknown)
know: party (and they never speak of it)
secrets.detonation: Ja'ha can enter any scene as Valerius agent wearing a friendly face. Seraleth is an open thread with a grudge.
